steps.title: Separate two complete clauses
steps.content: The sentence has two complete clauses: scholars needn't despair, and they can still procure another work by Varro. The transition though belongs with the first clause, and a semicolon can separate the two complete clauses.

steps.title: Reject comma-only joins
steps.content: Choice A leaves two complete clauses improperly joined. Choice B uses commas around though but still does not properly separate the clauses. Choice D uses an em dash but leaves the second clause unattached. The comma before though plus semicolon after it creates the needed boundary. The answer is C.
